name: Space and Satellite Systems (SSS) Club
relevance: An undergraduate student team developing CubeSat missions, including the REALOP-1 mission selected by NASA's CubeSat Launch Initiative.

name: Aggie Propulsion and Rocketry Lab (APRL)
relevance: A student-run organization focused on the design and construction of bi-propellant liquid-fueled rockets, aiming to launch the first student-built liquid rocket at UC Davis.

name: Aggie Space Initiative (ASI) - SEDS UC Davis
relevance: The UC Davis chapter of Students for the Exploration and Development of Space (SEDS), which competes in NASA design challenges like MITTIC.

name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA) at UC Davis
relevance: The UC Davis student chapter of AIAA, providing professional networking and design opportunities for students interested in aerospace.

name: UC Davis Center for Spaceflight Research (CSFR)
relevance: A transdisciplinary research center directed by former astronaut Stephen Robinson, coordinating spaceflight research across multiple laboratories.

name: Human/Robotics/Vehicle Integration and Performance (HRVIP) Laboratory
relevance: A laboratory within CSFR focusing on human-machine cooperation, spacecraft robotics, and the design of deep-space habitats.

name: Bioastronautics and eXploration Systems (BXS) Laboratory
relevance: Conducts interdisciplinary research on the physiology of human spaceflight and the design of systems for long-duration missions.

name: Complex Human-Physiological Systems (CHPS) Laboratory
relevance: Studies human physiological responses to the extreme conditions of spaceflight as part of the Center for Spaceflight Research.

name: Advanced Composites Research, Engineering and Science (ACRES) Laboratory
relevance: Focuses on the durability and mechanics of fiber-reinforced polymer composites for aerospace applications.

name: Planetary Science Research Group
relevance: An Earth and Planetary Sciences group studying the origin and evolution of planetary bodies in the solar system.

name: Shock Compression Laboratory
relevance: Directed by Sarah Stewart, this lab investigates the formation of planets through high-pressure shock physics experiments.

name: Yin Lab
relevance: Uses isotope geochemistry and meteoritics to study the age and formation of the Earth, Moon, and other solar system bodies.

name: Dawn Sumner's Research Group
relevance: Focuses on Martian geology and history as part of the NASA Mars Science Laboratory (Curiosity rover) science team.

name: Vertical Flight Society (VFS) - UC Davis
relevance: A student chapter focusing on VTOL technology and UAVs, with applications for planetary exploration like the Mars helicopter.

name: ECLIPSE Rocketry
relevance: A student-run team that historically competed in the NASA University Student Launch Initiative for solid motor rocketry.

name: High Altitude Ballooning Team
relevance: A UC Davis team selected as a finalist for the NASA Balloon Challenge to develop near-space atmospheric sensing platforms.

name: BovineWorks
relevance: A senior design team that won first place in a NASA-sponsored undergraduate aeronautics design challenge.

name: Remote Sensing and Ecosystem Change Lab
relevance: Headed by Prof. Yufang Jin, the lab uses satellite data for Earth observation and ecological monitoring.

name: National Association of Rocketry (NAR) at UC Davis
relevance: Provides certifications and launch opportunities for students interested in hobbyist and high-power rocketry.
